title: Amidst waving of party flags, an offbeat representation of the Flag above all flags
content: Against the backdrop of intense poll activity, a 400-metre stretch of an arterial road in the outskirts of Chennai (technically in Madurapakkam panchayat under Tambaram assembly constituency) presents a salute to the idea of res publica, which underpins India’s system of governance, where citizens have a say, at least before a government is securely ensconced in the seat of power.  A Republic Day initiative shines bright every night on a 400-metre stretch of a road in Kovilancherry

title: When drinking water became a revolution | Mahad Satyagraha
content:
A century ago, Ambedkar led a protest in Mahad that turned the right to water into a fight for dignity and equality.
When drinking water became an act of resistance, this is the story of the Mahad Satyagraha. 100 years after Mahad, Ambedkar’s fight for equality still resonates across India.

title: Misinformation wave deepens anxiety in Hyderabad, triggers panic buying and citywide disruption
content: In the past few days, rumours of an impending “lockdown” have spread swiftly across social media platforms, prompting residents to rush to grocery stores and fuel stations in a bid to stock up on essentials

title: Jaishankar in France for G7 meet; West Asia crisis tops agenda
content: The Foreign Ministers will deliberate on exploring different negotiation channels that could lead to de-escalation in the West Asia and reopen maritime routes, the official said

content: Harshavardhan Sapkal said that it is shocking to see such unethical behaviour of a public representative in whom people have invested their faith and money

title: India raises clean-energy ambition with 60% non-fossil fuel power goal by 2035
content: It also aims to reduce by 47% the intensity of emissions per unit of GDP and to increase its carbon sink to 3.5 billion tonnes-4 billion tonnes.

title: Only a structured party combined with popularity can reach success: Thousand Lights MLA N. Ezhilan
content: Thousand Lights MLA from the DMK N. Ezhilan, in an interview to The Hindu, observes that any popular personality entering politics will attract the limelight. But only a structured party machinery combined with popularity can ensure success. In this interview, he speaks about his tenure as first-time MLA from Thousand Lights Assembly constituency in Chennai, Vijay’s political entry shaping the 2026 elections, redressal of key grievances and more.

title: Four queer voices explain the impact of the Trans Amendment Bill 2026
content: The Transgender Amendment Bill which was passed in Lok Sabha on March 24 impacts a trans person’s ability to access better education, health care, and the right to self-determination, say gender-diverse members who took part in a nation-wide protest in Chennai on March 23. They strongly call for repealing the amendment

content: Rajendran was the brother-in-law of actor-MLA Mukesh. He was known for his versatile performances in Malayalam cinema and television

content: Official sources said the J&K government, unlike the Lieutenant Governor administration, intended to form a committee to look into the lease issues and auctions

content: The police said the missing man, described as wheatish in complexion and of medium build, was suffering from a mental health condition

content: To facilitate seamless travel, BMRCL said that IPL match tickets will include a QR code enabling two-way metro travel

content: According to the officials, the bus, belonging to Harikrishna Travels, was en route from Nirmal in Telangana to Nellore when the accident occurred

title: Bengaluru corporations plan night patrols, fix accountability to curb illegal banners
content: Among the major reasons behind the banner issue in the city are poor ground-level enforcement, lack of coordination, and the timing of banner installations

title: Private member bill seeks to establish central optical devices quality control and vision care regulation
content: The proposed bill seeks to offer remedy to the alleged vulnerability of consumers who according to the bill are in a “no-man’s land” between the manufacture of a lens and its delivery

title: Touted as cement hub, but for Ariyalur voters the future appears grey, thanks to multifaceted issues
content: Residents, who blame successive governments for failing to invest adequately and prioritise the district, feel that the constituency would have changed to a great extent if it had high-profile leaders

title: Toll system in Hormuz? Iran drafts bill to charge ships for safe passage amid war
content: The Middle East war has intensified an oil crisis as Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz disrupt global energy supplies.  While the Red Sea's Bab el-Mandeb Strait is considered an alternative, it also faces threats. Reopening Hormuz is complicated by its geography and requires significant security measures, including a multinational coalition, to overcome the risks of attacks and minefields.

title: Why IPL 2026 could unleash Virat Kohli’s most dangerous version yet
content: Virat Kohli, IPL's leading run-scorer, finally achieved his dream of winning the IPL trophy with Royal Challengers Bengaluru in 2025. Now, unburdened by expectations after relinquishing captaincy, he enters IPL 2026 with a rare sense of freedom, potentially unleashing his most dangerous form yet.

title: Indore horror: Techie killed as car rams residents in penthouse row
content: A dispute over commercial penthouse rentals in Indore's Sagar Township tragically turned deadly. A man allegedly drove his car into a group of residents during an argument, killing software engineer Shampa Pathak and injuring others. The conflict arose from residents' objections to short-term rentals causing security concerns. Both the driver and his father are in custody.

title: Microsoft HR head makes it clear to employees it's time to change
content: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman announced a significant HR restructuring, prioritizing adaptability and speed for the AI era. This shakeup includes leadership changes and new teams focused on accelerating HR operations, employee experience, and workforce skilling. The move signals a shift towards agility and a faster pace for the tech giant.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 8: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Dhurandhar 1' record of Rs 840
content: As Aditya Dhar’s espionage masterpiece Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge enters its eighth day on Thursday, March 26, 2026, the Ranveer Singh starrer is rewriting the history of Indian cinema. Having already shattered the Rs. 1,000 crore worldwide milestone in record time, the film is now tracking at nearly double the efficiency of previous blockbusters like 'Jawan', 'Pathaan', and 'Animal.' Despite its sprawling 3-hour and 49-minute runtime, the sequel's domestic net collection has surged to more than Rs. 630 crore, fueled by unprecedented demand for midnight screenings and a gripping narrative that sees Jaskirat Singh Rangi, played by Ranveer Singh deeper than ever into the Karachi underworld as Hamza Ali Mazari.

title: I eat non-veg even on Ugadi and Shivaratri: CM Siddaramaiah
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah sparked debate by stating he eats non-vegetarian food on festival days like Ugadi and Maha Shivaratri, asserting personal belief over ritual. He also explained presenting the budget before an inauspicious time was a family and official suggestion, a rare deviation from his usual disregard for such beliefs.

title: How will UDAN 2.0 scheme benefit consumers? Check key features of the aviation initiative
content: India's UDAN 2.0 scheme, with a Rs 28,000 crore outlay, aims to significantly boost regional air travel over 10 years. The revamped initiative will develop 100 airports, support aerodrome operations, build helipads, and provide financial aid to airlines, enhancing connectivity to Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.

title: Billionaire founder Mark Cuban makes an AI prediction that employees may like
content: Mark Cuban predicts a future where AI adoption allows companies to shorten workdays by an hour, maintaining current pay. He urges businesses to embrace AI immediately, warning that those who don't will falter. Cuban emphasizes understanding AI's capabilities and risks, likening its memory to a savant but noting its lack of true understanding.

title: Trump says he wants Iran war to end within weeks: Report
content: President Trump aims to conclude the Iran conflict within weeks, believing it's in its final stages. He has urged officials to adhere to a four-to-six-week timeline, potentially before a summit with China's Xi Jinping. White House states Iran expressed willingness to talk, leading to postponed strikes. However, Tehran insists hostilities will cease only on its terms, vowing continued defence

title: Vijay Mallya on RCB sale: ‘Will remain part of my DNA’, recalls Virat Kohli pick
content: Former RCB owner Vijay Mallya expressed his emotions following the franchise's record sale, stating it remains in his DNA. He recalled his initial INR 450 crore investment in 2008, which has now grown to an astounding INR 16,600 crore. Mallya highlighted his decision to pick a young Virat Kohli as a significant memory, thanking fans for their unwavering support.

title: OnePlus shutdown fears grow as India CEO exits: Here is what the company is saying
content: OnePlus India operations are under scrutiny following CEO Robin Liu's resignation. While the company assures business continuity, reports suggest a restructuring where Liu now reports to Realme's CEO. This comes after Liu previously denied shutdown rumors, and as OnePlus faces significant shipment declines in the Indian market, raising questions about its future.

title: Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 update teased: Launch tomorrow, what to expect
content: Royal Enfield is set to unveil an updated Guerrilla 450 on March 27, 2026. While the core design and 452cc engine remain, expect enhanced riding dynamics with grippier tires and revised suspension. New color options are likely, and features like traction and cruise control could be added. This refresh aims to boost its appeal.

title: ‘I’m back bowling’: Cummins eyes IPL return, backs Ishan Kishan as SRH captain
content: Australia captain Pat Cummins is targeting a mid-IPL 2026 return after resuming bowling following a back injury. He confirmed a structured rehabilitation plan aimed for his availability for the latter half of the tournament and finals. In his absence, Ishan Kishan will lead Sunrisers Hyderabad, a decision Cummins fully supports.

title: 7th case against astrologer: Woman accuses Ashok Kharat of rape on promise of bright career
content: Astrologer Ashok Kharat faces a seventh rape case in Nashik after a 24-year-old woman accused him of drugging and sexually assaulting her multiple times. Kharat allegedly threatened the victim with his 'divine powers' to ensure her silence. This latest complaint follows his arrest on March 17, with a special investigation team now probing further allegations.

title: 'Friendly nations' only: Iran allows India, Pakistan, 3 other countries to use Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran has opened the Strait of Hormuz for friendly nations like India, China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan. This comes as UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres urged an end to the conflict and the reopening of the vital waterway. The Strait of Hormuz is a crucial oil route, and its volatility impacts global energy flows amid regional tensions.

title: IPL opening ceremony in Bengaluru? BCCI takes big call before RCB vs SRH
content: In a poignant tribute to the 11 victims of last year's Bengaluru stampede, the BCCI has cancelled the IPL 2026 opening ceremony.  Instead, a grand closing ceremony is planned for the final on May 31. This decision marks a solemn remembrance of the tragedy that occurred during RCB's title celebrations, prioritizing respect over pre-match festivities.

title: US-Iran mediation: JD Vance might visit Pakistan over weekend to talk it out with Tehran
content: US vice president JD Vance might visit Pakistan this week for crucial talks aimed at de-escalating the Middle East conflict with Iran. This potential visit, part of backchannel diplomacy, follows indications that Iran prefers negotiating through Vance. Pakistan has offered to host these sensitive discussions, even as the US maintains military pressure.

title: ‘Lacks basic understanding’: Pinarayi Vijayan slams Rahul Gandhi, calls Congress ‘B-team’ of BJP
content: Keralam Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan labelled Rahul Gandhi and the Congress as the BJP's 'B-team', questioning Gandhi's understanding of Keralam politics. This exchange follows Gandhi's accusation of a hidden understanding between the LDF and BJP, and his criticism of the state government's job promises. Both parties accuse each other of not being accountable to the people.

title: Will gold rally to Rs 1.55 lakh/10 grams & silver touch Rs 2.60 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Gold and silver prices may sustain their up move in the coming sessions, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: Waking up with headache? Doctors explain silent triggers and simple habits to prevent it
content: Waking up shouldn't bring discomfort. If you're greeted by stubborn headaches each morning, it's a sign that something might be amiss, like inadequate slumber, insufficient hydration, or possibly sleep apnea. Health professionals recommend investigating these recurring pains since they could reveal troubling lifestyle choices that need attention.

title: BCCI warns IPL captains on catch rules, provides update on impact player rule
content: IPL captains have been warned about strict enforcement of fielder movement rules during catches, with officials emphasizing complete control before celebrations. The Impact Player rule will also remain for at least another season, despite concerns from players like Axar Patel about its impact on all-rounder development.

title: 'He'd rather die in prison': Imran Khan unbowed, sons share powerful message
content: Imran Khan, imprisoned for nearly three years, remains resolute, his sons reveal.  Despite health concerns and his wife's alleged mistreatment, Khan states authorities will never break him. He finds strength in meditation, viewing his incarceration as a trial.  His sons, awaiting visas, last spoke to him for 28 minutes, noting his unwavering principles.

title: All about Brahmanandam’s Hyderabad white themed home
content: Comedy legend Brahmanandam's Hyderabad residence in Manikonda is a testament to his artistic soul. Featuring pristine white tile flooring and walls adorned with his own paintings and sculptures, the home blends modern and traditional aesthetics.  This lavish abode, often shared on social media, reflects the actor's creative spirit beyond his iconic film career.

title: Video: Bus falls off ferry into Padma River in Bangladesh; 23 dead
content: Tragedy struck Bangladesh as a passenger bus plunged into the Padma River, claiming at least 23 lives. Rescue efforts are ongoing to find missing individuals after the bus fell from a ferry near Daulatdia. Officials confirmed 23 bodies recovered, with some survivors reaching safety. The submerged bus was eventually lifted hours later.

title: 85 plus year Harvard study reveals people with this kind of job are the unhappiest
content: A 85-year Harvard study reveals that emotionally isolating jobs lead to unhappiness, regardless of pay or hours.  Lack of genuine human connection leaves employees feeling empty and exhausted.  Positive workplace relationships are crucial for job satisfaction, better performance, and overall well-being, proving to be a vital component of a fulfilling work life.

title: Designer accused of domestic violence, arrested by authorities
content: In a shocking turn of events, rapper Desiigner, famed for his hit 'Panda', found himself in handcuffs in South Carolina on Monday, facing charges of third-degree domestic violence. Reports suggest a heated encounter involving both physical confrontation and property destruction, along with damage to the victim's clothing.

title: ‘Trump doesn’t bluff’: WH warns Iran, says ‘will unleash hell’ against 'any miscalculation'
content: The White House issued a stern warning to Iran, stating President Trump is prepared to "unleash hell" if Tehran rejects a deal to end the Middle East conflict. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt asserted that Iran has been militarily defeated and will face intensified strikes if it fails to accept this reality.

title: CBSE Class 10 result 2026: Expected date, past 5-year trends
content: CBSE Class 10 results for 2026 are anticipated soon, likely mid-May, mirroring past trends. Students can check scores on cbse.gov.in or results.cbse.nic.in, and via DigiLocker. Essential details like roll number and date of birth are needed. The board is expected to return to its usual mid-May release schedule after pandemic-induced delays in previous years.

title: Digital addiction in children: How concerned should parents be, and the early warning signs?
content: Digital addiction is a growing concern for parents as children spend excessive hours on devices, impacting physical and mental health. Experts urge parents to set screen time limits, encourage outdoor play, and engage in family activities. Recognizing warning signs like irritability and loss of interest in hobbies is crucial to foster healthy habits and improve children's well-being.

title: ‘Not one, but two Sheesh Mahals’: CAG report dominates Delhi assembly business
